content: I got chopped up again trading the momentum setup during the CPI spike. High-volatility opens keep invalidating the breakout before I can manage risk.
Noted. The repeated failure pattern is momentum entries during high volatility, especially around macro releases and opening range noise.
Yeah, when VIX is elevated I still reach for the same playbook. I end up entering too early and widening stops after the first fakeout.
Durable observation: you are currently treating momentum as the default approach even when volatility regime suggests mean reversion or waiting. Noise note: you also mentioned your coffee went cold.
Please remember that my main issue is not signal quality, it is applying the strategy in the wrong regime. The coffee and the red keyboard backlight are irrelevant.
Captured: momentum strategy underperforms for you in high-volatility conditions; regime filtering and patience at the open appear important. Ignoring filler about desk setup.
I want to shift to a slower confirmation-based entry process on volatile mornings instead of forcing the breakout. Also, my lunch order was wrong again.
Understood. Durable items are the high-volatility momentum failure, early entries after fakeouts, and a current move toward slower confirmation-based entries. Lunch order is noise.
Exactly. If a setup depends on immediate continuation during unstable price discovery, I should probably stand down or downgrade size.
